shared.caveats:
A household is in relative low income if they have less than 60% of the income of the average (median) household in the UK in a particular year. An annual survey of around 20,000 households estimates how many children (in the UK) live in households with an income below this level. Administrative data on benefits and earnings is then used to estimate how many children for the UK wide estimate live in each local authority in the UK.

Figures are provisional and may be subject to revision in subsequent releases.

Rates (presented as percentages) have been derived by dividing the number of children aged 0 to 15 living in low income families by the total number of children aged 0 to 15. Population figures are sourced from the mid‑2024 population estimates.

Further information is available in the [Background information and methodology for Children in low income families: local area statistics](https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/children-in-low-income-families-local-area-statistics-background-information-and-methodology/background-information-and-methodology-children-in-low-income-families-local-area-statistics).

Some of this data has been aggregated and may show differences to other published figures.

Children in relative poverty before hous.label: Children in relative poverty before housing costs
Children in relative poverty before hous.subtitle: Percentage of children aged under 16 living in relative low income families Before Housing Costs (BHC)
Children in relative poverty before hous.longDescription: This indicator shows the percentage of children aged under 16 living in relative low income families Before Housing Costs (BHC), in the UK, for financial years ending 2022 to 2025.

Children in relative poverty after housi.label: Children in relative poverty after housing costs
Children in relative poverty after housi.subtitle: Percentage of children aged under 16 living in relative low income families After Housing Costs (AHC)
Children in relative poverty after housi.longDescription: This indicator shows the percentage of children aged under 16 living in relative low income families After Housing Costs (AHC), in the UK, for financial years ending 2024 and 2025.
